title: psoralea bituminosa subsp. plumosa
intro: Psoralea bituminosa subsp. plumosa, also known as the plum psoralea, is a plant species in the legume family. It is a perennial herb native to South Africa, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains coumarins and other compounds, which have been found to possess antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, antifungal, and antibacterial effects. Psoralea bituminosa subsp. plumosa can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been used in traditional herbal remedies for various ailments.
